rulings.Case Title: DEPOSITED WILL OF RICHARD C. RAACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TRANSFER to SAN DIEGO County
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues were e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/2020. Attorney still has not filed a response to the Examiner's notes. 1) Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. 2) The Petition does not say how much additional time is requested to complete the estate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH WHALLEY-KENDRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/20. The register of actions does not reflect a Final I&A. (No copies of the I&As were provided; reviewing the gains and losses on sale is very time-consuming.) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the legal description of real property, the complete description of each individual security, and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.67 L.7.] (Attachment 1 does not show the proposed distribution.) Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Costs advanced are not set forth in the petition in detail and therefore will not be confirmed and approved. A courtesy copy of any responsive pleading must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/16/20.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JUSTIN WINFREY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of extending appointment of Special Administrator, if necessary. Counsel should provide a proposed order extending Letters of Special Administration (although no Letters of Special Administration have been issued yet) if a Special Administrator is still necessary. The Petition for Appointment of Administrator is NOT ready to be heard for the following reasons: No proof of service or proof of publication of notice has been filed. The petition caption is incomplete if IAEA authority is being sought. Item 2b of the petition shows that the person to be appointed is "to be determined" and item 3g(2)(c) also shows the relationship as "to be determined." The petitioner does not have priority. (She has also not yet obtained her Letters of Special Administration.) Item 5b of the petition is incomplete. It appears that the petitioner IS the person seeking appointment and should have been the person named in item 2 of the petition; she is missing from item 8. An amended petition must be filed to cure the defects. After the amended petition is filed, notice of the amended petition must be served and published. [CRC 7.53(a).] If the amended petition cannot be filed in time to serve and publish notice of the hearing on 11/24/20, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request a continuance.
